title: NVIDIA 800 VDC Architecture Will Power the Next Generation of AI Factories
text: NVIDIA is spearheading a transition to 800-volt direct current (VDC) power infrastructure designed to support megawatt-scale computing racks beginning in 2027. This architectural shift addresses the exponential growth of AI workloads that current power systems cannot efficiently handle. Traditional 54 VDC in-rack power distribution was engineered for kilowatt-scale operations. As data centers move toward MW-scale racks, three critical challenges emerge: Space limitations requiring up to 64 U of rack space for power shelves alone, eliminating room for compute resources; Material intensity requiring approximately 200,000 kg of copper for a single 1 GW data center; and Conversion inefficiency from multiple AC/DC transformations throughout the power chain. The new 800 VDC system converts grid power directly to 800 VDC at the data center perimeter using industrial rectifiers, then distributes this voltage through facility-wide busways to individual racks. Higher voltage reduces current demand, lowering resistive losses and enabling 85% more power to be transmitted through the same conductor size. The architecture delivers measurable benefits: Efficiency gain of up to 5% improvement in end-to-end power efficiency, Maintenance reduction of up to 70% decrease in maintenance costs through fewer power supply failures, Copper reduction of 45% less copper required versus traditional approaches, and Total cost of ownership of potential 30% reduction overall. NVIDIA collaborates with silicon manufacturers, power component suppliers, and systems providers to standardize this transition. Full-scale production deployment aligns with NVIDIA's Kyber rack-scale systems in 2027.
